svg.text: VLLM Benchmark Comparison: 2k vs 10k Input Context
Same endpoint and output length; comparison date 2026-03-30 14:58:41
10k input pushes latency up at every stable concurrency
Timeout cliff appears much earlier for 10k input
Practical throughput peak shifts lower under 10k input
Longer prompts increase total tok/s but reduce stable concurrency headroom
1. 2k input remains stable through concurrency 80, while 10k input saturates before 80.
2. At concurrency 40, mean latency rises from 83.24s to 114.46s when input grows from 2k to 10k.
3. At concurrency 80, 2k still has 0% timeout and 0.65 req/s, but 10k becomes 100% timeout.
4. 10k input improves total successful tok/s at low stable concurrency, but loses queueing headroom quickly.
5. For production, 10k input should keep concurrency at or below 40 under this timeout budget.
